name: DOGE Range Failure Reacceptance v1
edge_mechanism: range-failure mean reversion after displacement loses acceptance and re-enters balance
thesis: DOGE 4h is the best home lane for testing whether repeated balance rejections can be harvested when a displacement away from fast value fails and price re-accepts balance. The edge is not breakout prediction; it is betting that overextended moves in a chop-heavy transition regime snap back toward value often enough to survive costs.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reacceptance events still come too infrequently, if reversion travel is too shallow after costs, or if DOGE transitions into one-sided trend persistence that punishes balance bets.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price stretches away from short-term balance, fails to keep directional acceptance, closes back through fast value, and mean-reverts toward deeper balance before the next impulse.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: The active DOGE lane is dominated by chop with intermittent transition. In that environment, failed excursions should outnumber clean trend continuations, making balance reacceptance denser than strict breakout logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is appropriate because it is expressive enough to overshoot balance repeatedly, but still slow enough for a multi-bar reacceptance and time-based exit to matter.

indicator_role_map.RSI_14: stretch detector rather than trend confirmer
indicator_role_map.ADX_14: trend-strength veto to avoid betting against true expansion

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: A real trend may begin immediately after the reacceptance print, turning the supposed failure into an early continuation leg against the trade.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is fine but expectancy is weak, tighten the time stop and reduce target ambition before adding more filters.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Many small-to-medium rotations back toward balance, with edge carried by high participation and quick exits rather than long runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: Screen output shows credible density, DOGE produces repeated snap-backs toward EMA_55, and at least one cross-lane preserves the same reacceptance behavior.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trade screen output, or repeated entries that immediately re-break away from balance.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: low; stretch, reacceptance, and trend-strength veto are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept low by using broad displacement from EMA_20 instead of ceremonial breakout-hold choreography

validation_basket.why_included: Most natural lane for failed excursion and balance reacceptance in the allowed basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE trades with credible density and shows repeatable mean reversion back toward deeper balance.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE behaves like one-way trend persistence or the reacceptance leg is too shallow after costs.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Frequent reacceptance trades with modest but repeatable travel back toward EMA_55.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same balance-failure mechanism survives on a cleaner major rather than only on DOGE noise.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows the same snap-back logic with fewer but cleaner trades.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The thesis only exists on DOGE and disappears on a cleaner lane.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower density than DOGE but a cleaner reversion profile.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism survives on a higher-volatility structurally different asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO preserves the same failure-to-balance snap-back structure without devolving into noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO turns the setup into chaotic overshoot with no orderly reacceptance.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger balance snap-backs than DOGE.

long: Exit remaining 15% on touch of EMA_55 or 3 bars
short: Exit remaining 15% on touch of EMA_55 or 3 bars
